blocks.content: Maya arrived at two, which meant she'd been thinking about it all morning.

She brought coffee — which meant she was trying — and sat at the kitchen table with her hands wrapped around her cup and looked at Cassandra with the quiet, thorough attention of someone who loves you so much it sometimes looks like suspicion.

blocks.speaker: Maya
blocks.content: You look okay.

blocks.speaker: Cassandra
blocks.content: Thank you.

blocks.speaker: Maya
blocks.content: Tell me about him.
No warm-up. Straight to it — that was Maya.

blocks.speaker: Cassandra
blocks.content: He's an artist. He's quiet, but not in a way that feels like absence. He notices things. He listens.

blocks.speaker: Maya
blocks.content: How happy are you around him?

blocks.speaker: Cassandra
blocks.content: Very.
Maya set her cup down.

blocks.speaker: Maya
blocks.content: How long has this been going on?

blocks.speaker: Cassandra
blocks.content: A few weeks.

blocks.speaker: Maya
blocks.content: And he still doesn't know.
Again — not a question.

blocks.speaker: Cassandra
blocks.content: No.

blocks.speaker: Maya
blocks.content: So he's falling for you without the full picture. He has no idea what he's actually getting into.
Cassandra felt the shift. This wasn't the usual conversation — the careful, measured version she'd been having with Maya for years. Something in her sister was wound tighter today.

blocks.speaker: Cassandra
blocks.content: I'm going to tell him. When the time is right.

blocks.speaker: Maya
blocks.content: Is that fair to him? Letting him get attached to someone who hasn't given him the information he needs to make that choice?

blocks.speaker: Cassandra
blocks.content: I don't lead with my medical history, Maya.

blocks.speaker: Maya
blocks.content: This isn't medical history. This is—
She stopped. Took a breath.

blocks.speaker: Maya
blocks.content: This is something that affects him. The longer you wait, the deeper he's in, and the less choice he actually has.

blocks.speaker: Cassandra
blocks.content: I have the right to tell him when I'm ready.

blocks.speaker: Maya
blocks.content: And what about his right? What about what you're asking him to walk into without knowing?
That landed differently. Cassandra had expected the usual — the concern turned inward, the protect-yourself argument. This was different. Maya was talking about Ethan.

blocks.speaker: Cassandra
blocks.content: I'm not asking him to do anything yet.

blocks.speaker: Maya
blocks.content: Not yet. But you will be. And by then he'll already love you and he won't be able to walk away even if he would have chosen differently if he'd known from the start.
The kitchen was very quiet.

blocks.speaker: Cassandra
blocks.content: You don't know that.

blocks.speaker: Maya
blocks.content: You don't either. That's the whole point.
Cassandra looked at her sister. Maya's jaw was tight. Her hands were wrapped around her coffee cup like she needed something to hold onto.
This wasn't just about Ethan.
This had never been just about Ethan.
